evals.prompt: I've been putting together docs for TaskFlow and I want to make sure they're all telling the same story before I share them with the team. Can you check these three files for me? /coherency-check evals/files/test-1-project-docs/
evals.expected_output: A coherency report identifying multiple contradictions: Node.js version (18 vs 20), framework (Express vs Fastify), PostgreSQL version (14 vs 15), auth method (JWT vs session-based), token/session duration (24h vs 8h), workspace size limit (10 vs 25), task statuses (4-status vs 3-status vs 4-different-status), rate limits (100/min vs 60/min), auth header (Bearer vs X-API-Key), deployment target (AWS ECS vs GCP Cloud Run), IaC tool (Terraform vs Pulumi), infrastructure directory (infrastructure/ vs deploy/), and dev server port (3000 vs 8080). Should then ask the user to resolve each contradiction.
expectations: Report identifies the Node.js version contradiction (18 vs 20)
Report identifies the framework contradiction (Express vs Fastify)
Report identifies the authentication method contradiction (JWT vs session-based)
Report identifies the task status values contradiction (at least 2 of the 3 conflicting definitions)
Report identifies the rate limit contradiction (100/min vs 60/min)
Report identifies the deployment target contradiction (AWS vs GCP)
Each contradiction includes specific file names and quoted values
Report assigns severity levels to contradictions
After the report, the skill offers to walk through resolution of each contradiction

evals.prompt: We've got an API spec, SDK docs, and a changelog that might have gotten out of sync. Can you cross-check them? /coherency-check evals/files/test-2-api-spec/
evals.expected_output: A coherency report catching: token expiry (1h vs 24h), account deletion behaviour (soft-delete with 30-day recovery vs permanent/immediate — with the changelog complicating things by showing a 14-day window), login rate limit (5 vs 10 per minute), pagination max (50 vs 100 per page with changelog confirming change), project colour format (hex-only vs any CSS value — changelog confirms hex-only as current), email verification (required vs removed per changelog), project limit (10 free-tier vs unlimited). Should flag that the changelog resolves some contradictions by showing which value is current.
expectations: Report identifies the token expiry contradiction (1 hour vs 24 hours)
Report identifies the account deletion behaviour contradiction (soft-delete vs permanent)
Report identifies the login rate limit contradiction (5 vs 10 attempts per minute)
Report identifies the project limit contradiction (10 free-tier vs unlimited)
Report uses the changelog to inform which contradictions have a clear resolution
Report identifies the colour/color format contradiction (hex-only vs any CSS value)
Each finding includes specific file references and quoted values
After the report, the skill offers to resolve contradictions interactively

evals.prompt: Just finished writing the vision, spec, and roadmap for Metronome. Before I hand these off, can you make sure they're all coherent? /coherency-check evals/files/test-3-clean-docs/
evals.expected_output: A clean bill of health — all three documents are consistent. Tempo range, time signatures, presets, and feature set align across all files. The report should confirm this without fabricating issues.
expectations: Report confirms no contradictions were found
Report does NOT fabricate issues that don't exist in the documents
Report lists the files that were checked
Report is concise — does not pad a clean result with unnecessary detail

evals.prompt: I'm about to share the Pulse project docs with a new dev joining the team. Can you check these three files are consistent? /coherency-check evals/files/test-4-research-resolve/
evals.expected_output: Report finds contradictions in two categories: (1) factual contradictions about real technologies — getServerSideProps vs Server Components in App Router, Tailwind v4 config format, useEffect timing — each resolved by researching official docs and citing the source URL, and (2) preference contradictions — package manager (pnpm vs yarn), deployment target (Amplify vs Vercel), API design (REST vs Server Actions), rate limit (200 vs 100) — presented to the user for decision.
expectations: Report identifies the getServerSideProps vs Server Components contradiction
Skill researches official Next.js docs to resolve the App Router data-fetching contradiction
Report identifies the Tailwind v4 config format contradiction (JS file vs CSS @theme)
Skill researches official Tailwind docs to resolve the config format contradiction
Report identifies the useEffect timing contradiction (before vs after paint)
Skill researches official React docs to resolve the useEffect timing contradiction
Each researched resolution includes a source URL from official documentation
Report identifies the package manager contradiction (pnpm vs yarn) as a preference
Report identifies the deployment target contradiction (Amplify vs Vercel) as a preference
Preference contradictions are presented to the user for decision, not auto-resolved
Factual and preference contradictions are clearly separated in the output
